9:["$","section",null,{"className":"mt-16 bg-stone-800 text-stone-100 rounded-lg p-8 md:p-12","children":[["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-3xl font-bold mb-6 text-center font-[family-name:var(--font-playfair)]","children":"The Thesis"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"max-w-3xl mx-auto text-lg leading-relaxed space-y-4","children":[["$","p",null,{"children":"While history remembers Alexander as \"the Great,\" a critical examination reveals that his achievements were built almost entirely upon the foundations laid by his father, Philip II of Macedon. From the revolutionary phalanx formation to a professional standing army and a treasury flush with gold, Alexander inherited the tools of conquest rather than forging them himself."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"This educational resource examines the evidence that challenges Alexander's traditional narrative, comparing his inherited empire to those built from scratch by leaders like Napoleon Bonaparte."}]]}]]}]
